Land Rover Discovery 5.0 V8 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery 5.0 V8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Discovery 5.0 V8 engine (AJ133) typically costs £6,000–£8,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,400–£2,100. These are specialist-only replacements — the AJ133 requires Land Rover or Jaguar specialist expertise. A timing chain job alone costs £2,000–£4,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Discovery 5.0 V8 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ133 5.0 V8 is fundamentally robust and characterful, but early examples (2010–2012) are known for timing chain guide wear. The single most important factor is oil change frequency — 5,000–7,500 mile intervals are essential to prevent chain issues. JLR revised the guide design in 2015, so post-2015 cars are more reliable from new. The plastic coolant crossover pipes are also a known weakness.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Discovery 5.0 V8?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain guide wear on pre-2015 models (rattling on startup, £2,000–£4,000 repair), plastic coolant crossover pipe failure (coolant loss, overheating, £800–£2,000 repair), and carbon build-up on intake valves due to direct injection (rough idle, reduced power, £400–£800 for walnut blasting).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery 5.0 V8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Discovery 4 values range from £6,000–£18,000 depending on condition and mileage. A £7,000–£8,000 reconditioned engine replacement represents 40–60% of vehicle value on lower-value examples — our 60% Land Rover scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement is often justified. For a higher-mileage example with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Discovery 5.0 V8 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Discovery 4 5.0 V8 uses a single engine code: AJ133 (sometimes listed as 508PN or LR-V8 Gen III). This is Jaguar Land Rover's third-generation AJ-V8, introduced in 2009. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and guide replacement costs £2,000–£4,000 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£5,000–£8,500). The job requires approximately 18.4 hours of labour, making it a major but worthwhile investment on a well-maintained Discovery. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic failure.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Discovery 5.0 V8 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the Discovery 4 L319 5.0 V8 — a single-generation engine. All pricing is indicative; actual costs vary by mileage, condition and workshop location.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Discovery 5.0 V8 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £80–£120/hr (independent Land Rover specialist) or £140–£190/hr (London/SE dealer). A timing chain job alone calls for approximately 18.4 hours of book labour.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery 5.0 V8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative replacement before failure — always cheaper than engine replacement
Fit aftermarket metal pipes for a permanent fix
Regular maintenance item on direct-injection engines
Straightforward repair on an otherwise healthy engine
Replacement often £5,000–£8,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Discovery 5.0 V8? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2015 build with revised timing chain guides (steel inserts fitted)
* Documented timing chain replacement already completed
* Coolant crossover pipes replaced with metal aftermarket units
* Full service history with oil changes every 5,000–7,500 miles
avoidIf: * Pre-2015 model with no timing chain history (guide wear risk is high)
* Any evidence of coolant loss or overheating (crossover pipe failure risk)
* Poor service history — the AJ133 is unforgiving of extended oil change intervals
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive problems

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 5.0 V8 is one of the most characterful engines ever fitted to a Discovery — smooth, powerful and with a wonderful V8 soundtrack. The AJ133 is fundamentally robust, but early examples demand timing chain vigilance and cooling system awareness. A post-2015 car with a documented service history and metal coolant pipes is a genuine gem. Buy with your eyes open, maintain religiously, and this engine will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery 5.0 V8 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery 5.0 V8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain guide wear warning)
* Service History — Oil change intervals are critical — 5,000–7,500 miles is the safe zone
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss; inspect around crossover pipes for leaks
* Engine Performance — Test acceleration; any hesitation, flat spots, or rough idle?
* Carbon Build-up — Has the intake had a walnut blast service? (every 60k–80k miles)
* Oil Consumption — Monitor between services; AJ133s can consume oil in hard use
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (catalyst/sensor issues)

Actual costs vary by variant, engine code, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited-mileage warranty — always confirm terms with your chosen supplier.
